steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The text says gas stations near schools offer refueling discounts, while gas stations near restaurants do not. It asks why the good-to-bad comment ratio is lower for gas stations near restaurants.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The number of people going to gas stations near restaurants is greater than that of people going to gas stations near schools.: This compares customer volume, but the passage is about the ratio of good comments to bad comments, not how many customers visit each location. ✗
  • Choice B — People who go to gas stations near restaurants have higher expectations about the service because of higher prices.: This directly explains the pattern: without a discount, customers expect more from the service, so they may leave fewer good comments relative to bad ones. ✓
  • Choice C — People are more willing to give good comments to gas stations near schools.: This restates the pattern without explaining why customers near schools leave a better good-to-bad comment ratio. ✗
  • Choice D — More gas stations should be established near schools.: This is an unsupported policy proposal, not an explanation of the comment-ratio difference. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
